subject: Chemistry
title: Technetium: The First Artificial Element
description: A 60-second lesson on Technetium: The First Artificial Element.

hook.text: Element 43 was a gap in the table for decades. Every claim of discovery turned out to be wrong.

buildup.text: Mendeleev predicted it. Chemists searched in ores and minerals worldwide. Nothing.

discovery.text: In 1937, Carlo Perrier and Emilio Segrè found it in a discarded piece of a cyclotron from California.

twist.text: They named it technetium—from 'technetos,' Greek for 'artificial.' It was the first element made by humans.

climax.text: Technetium is now used in 30 million medical scans per year. The artificial element saves real lives.

punchline.text: Nature never made it. Humans did. Now it saves millions of lives.

quiz.question: Why is technetium called 'artificial'?
options: It was the first element created by humans, not found in nature
It is made entirely of synthetic polymers
It can only exist in a laboratory vacuum
